title: cinchona micrantha
intro: Cinchona micrantha is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains a high concentration of quinine, a compound used to treat malaria and other fever-related illnesses. The health benefits of Cinchona micrantha include its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, making it effective in reducing fever and alleviating symptoms associated with malaria. Cinchona micrantha is traditionally prepared as a tea or tincture, and its bark can also be used to make a medicinal infusion, showcasing its versatility as a herbal remedy.
